GAC
Junior
Software Engineer / Business Analyst
Built
custom self-contained applications using PowerApps and Azure DevOps to revamp case-management processes for clients.

Created dynamic dashboards for every custom application using Power BI to visualize real-time operational insights for embassy officials and GAC leadership. These dashboards covered 50+ reporting metrics and enabled faster data-driven decision-making.
Upgraded application’s DevOps cloud pipeline infrastructure using custom TypeScript to automate environment variable generation.
Elicited requirements from
clients to gather feedback, assess
capabilities
, and iteratively improve software projects, ensuring 100% (yes, 100%) customer satisfaction upon project completion through continuous enhancements and alignment with client needs.

Designed system architecture, optimizing design decisions to meet system and requirement constraints
Optimized and refactored existing scripts improving efficiency by up to 7200%

and (experienced) testers
, serving as their primary source of assistance

Power BI
reports to visualize data

Lockheed Martin
Software Engineering Intern
Wrote

4 original scripts using DXL and C to facilitate version control management for a military vehicle design phase. These scripts are used by 50+ engineers and decrease the time to change the design’s baseline documentation from 2 hours to 10 minutes.

Encrypted 1000+ relational database objects into classified excel documents via a Python formatting script that converted SQL data into readable documents satisfying customer requirements and expectations.
Improved the database architecture for 3000+ objects by automating attribute linking, thereby saving hours of manual work per week.
Technical
Program Management Intern
Visualized 30+ weapon program KPIs (150+ parameters) using Jira API connector onto 20+ Tableau and Jira dashboards.
Developed 20+ pages of documentation on Agile methodologies on Confluence for engineers spanning a 12-figure weapon program.
Refactored 50+ program milestones in Jira to improve customer requirements delivery efficiency whilst meeting 100+ design requirements.
Authored documentation regarding Agile tools and processes, assisted 200+ engineers by streamlining information channels by 170%.

Coached internal engineering and non-engineering teams on Agile Scrum methodology by providing training and support to Scrum Masters, Product Owners, and Engineers. Resolved conflict and provided clarification regarding Agile wherever necessary.
Constructed dozens of Jira queries daily for data metrics, and assessments on statistics regarding ticket progression.

Lead meetings for Scrum Masters from multiple teams in the form of Agile Release Trains, and Scrum of Scrum meetings. These meetings continuously tracked and analyzed progress made on different teams and made sure development efforts were aligned.
S
ystems Engineering Intern
Designed 3 intricate system data models using CAMEO (UML) for model architecture and certification process visualization.
Iterated 50+ data artifacts with senior engineers for project milestones using tools such as PTC Windchill and IBM DOORS.

Tailored qualitative engineering reviews on Design Appraisal Documents, Engineering Change Requests, Procurement Packages, Clearance Requirements, Project Implementation Proposals, and other design specifications using Microsoft Office, PTC Windchill, and IBM DOORS.
o

Developed 10+ layered and complex data models for integrated systems within CAMEO modeling software using SysML, a variant of UML. These models illustrate the inner systems of military vehicles for the Department of Defense.

Conducted qualitative engineering reviews on Design Appraisal Documents, Engineering Change Requests, Procurement Packages, Clearance Requirements, Project Implementation Proposals, and other design specifications.
Collaborated
with senior engineers and utilized tools such as JIRA/Confluence, PTC Windchill, and IBM DOORS.

Engineered research and development efforts for the Canadian Surface Combatant - updated 90+ classified design documentations by performing requirements analyses, reviewing trades studies, and consulting vendors.
En Ville
Storefront Supervisor – (Toronto, ON)
En Ville (Est. 1982) is a high-end, premiere catering company based in Toronto, Canada
Oversaw daily operations of En Ville’s direct-to-consumer storefront, serving 80–100 customers per day during peak months
Boosted weekly sales by ~25% over three months through improved product layout and upselling high-margin items
Increased customer return rate by ~30% by encouraging feedback, offering samples, and refining product mix
Trained 3 new part-time staff, creating simple SOPs to ensure consistent service and reduce onboarding time
Managed restocking and inventory for 100+ SKUs; minimized product shortages and improved freshness
Handled customer issues calmly and professionally, resolving 20+ escalations with positive follow-up outcomes

Sales Associate – (Vaughan, ON)
GAC owns and operates games, activities, and attractions inside popular amusement parks i.e., Canada’s Wonderland
Learned an “impossible-to-do” carnival game quickly, and repeatedly demonstrated activity on daily basis.
Displayed consistent technical knowledge by repeatedly demonstrating game requirements to prospective customers.
Showed up every day on time, actively engaged over a thousand individuals per day, and improved generation of sales.

Co-Chair
– (Kingston, ON)

2025 – Present

ntroduced new hybrid event model and redesigned team onboarding, increasing participant satisfaction scores by 35%

May
2025 – Present
Represented 2000+ students in discussions with the undergraduate board, arts & sciences board, and school faculty.
Managed a team of 50+ students to bring 15+ professional development and community events to 2000+ students.
Advocated for and secured a $12,000 annual increase in student funding, the largest raise in over a decade
Designed and implemented COMPSA’s first standardized training system; boosted internal retention by 45% and cut onboarding time in half
Spearheaded faculty-wide survey campaigns (700+ responses); results led to new course offerings and mental health service changes

Apr
Managed team and efforts responsible for 2 large scale student events centered around business and technology, netting 200+ attendees.

May
Apr
Directed a team of 5 people in the scheduling, budgeting, and coordination efforts for 4 successful events totaling 300+ attendees.

correspondence with 10+ external stakeholders, vendors, and student organizations for event purposes.

Developed and implemented comprehensive logistics timelines, effectively coordinating with cross-functional teams and external vendors to ensure timely delivery of services and resources for planned events.
Managed the entirety of the club’s financial portfolio – acting as head Treasurer.
Negotiated with venues and sponsors months in advance of events to ensure efficiency.

Conducted post-event evaluations and analysis, gathering feedback from stakeholders, attendees, and team members to identify areas of improvement and implement actionable recommendations for future conferences.

May
Apr
Delivered 3 workshops on NLP, equipping 50+ students with hands-on experience in implementing AI models.
Spearheaded partnership communication with other clubs for AI education opportunities i.e. speaker panels, guest talks, workshops.
Founded

QDAA’s Artificial Intelligence (AI) division – focusing on bringing attention to the cross-section between Data Analytics and Artificial Intelligence. Researched Natural-Language-Processing (NLP) and Supervised Learning techniques.

Hired Senior and Junior AI Consultants to assist me in generating in-house educational content about AI, and in consulting QDAA project teams on creating Machine Learning solutions to existing problems.
Spearheaded partnership efforts with other campus organizations regarding collaborative education opportunities.
Organized educational workshops with internal design teams re. implementation of Supervised Learning in projects.
Deliberated with QDAA external clients on providing AI solutions to real-life business problems.

Aug

and maintained QSIG’s independent website (Check it out: QSIG).

Developed a security classification model in Python using Yahoo Finance API to predict stock performance – averaged 73% accuracy.
Programmed a reinforcement learning model to train AI with an LSTM model using PyTorch, TensorFlow, Colab.

Sep
Dec 2023
QBB is a financial/economic consulting chapter of Global Brigades, a non-profit organization supporting underdeveloped regions

Sep
May

QUANTT – Algorithm Team Software Developer – (Kingston, ON)
Sep
Aug
QUANTT is Canada’s premier student-run club focused on quantitative finance and algorithmic trading

Developed an algorithm in Python to trade securities using preset indicators – averaging 82% return on investment. The algorithm was built inside QuantConnect, a professional trading simulation that tracks real-time market movements.
Tested and improved the algorithm’s machine learning capabilities using backtesting and previous market data.
Inversely traded Oil Equities against Renewable Energy Equities to net stable, positive returns.
